prompt: For a protein shake, does drinking speed matter (chugging vs sipping)?
expected_output: SBS said speed of shake consumption is not a key lever in real-world mixed-meal contexts. Fiber, fat, meal composition, and GI transit all affect amino-acid appearance more than sip speed precision. The practical target remained daily distribution: roughly 3-5 protein feedings/boluses across the day. So lifters should focus on total intake and broad meal spacing, not micromanaging shake-drinking pace.
assertions: Answer states drinking speed of protein shakes is not a key lever in mixed-meal contexts
Answer mentions fiber, fat, meal composition affect amino acid appearance more than sip speed
Answer recommends focusing on daily distribution of roughly 3-5 protein feedings
